questions.q: Hampi is the site of the ruined capital of which empire?
questions.explanation: Hampi was the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire, one of the greatest Hindu kingdoms of southern India.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Hampi located?
questions.explanation: Hampi lies in the Ballari (Bellary) region of the state of Karnataka.

questions.q: On the banks of which river do the ruins of Hampi stand?
questions.explanation: Hampi sits on the southern bank of the Tungabhadra River in Karnataka.

questions.q: What is Hampi's status with UNESCO?
questions.explanation: The Group of Monuments at Hampi was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1986.

questions.q: Which famous ancient temple at Hampi remains an active place of worship today?
questions.explanation: The Virupaksha Temple, dedicated to a form of Shiva, is still a functioning pilgrimage shrine at Hampi.

questions.q: The Virupaksha Temple at Hampi is dedicated to which Hindu deity?
questions.explanation: Virupaksha is a form of Lord Shiva, and the temple is one of Hampi's holiest shrines.

questions.q: Hampi's most iconic emblem, a small shrine shaped like a chariot, is found in which temple complex?
questions.explanation: The celebrated Stone Chariot stands in the Vittala Temple complex at Hampi.

questions.q: What kind of place was Hampi at the height of the Vijayanagara Empire?
questions.explanation: Hampi was the prosperous capital of Vijayanagara, famed for its temples, palaces and bustling bazaars.

questions.q: Which kind of building material dominates the temples and structures at Hampi?
questions.explanation: Hampi's monuments were carved largely from the granite that is abundant in its boulder-strewn landscape.

questions.q: Hampi is famous for its dramatic natural landscape filled with what feature?
questions.explanation: Hampi sits among an extraordinary landscape of huge granite boulders and rocky hills.

questions.q: In which century was the Vijayanagara Empire founded, establishing its capital at Hampi?
questions.explanation: The Vijayanagara Empire was founded in 1336 CE, in the 14th century, with Hampi as its capital.

questions.q: Two brothers are traditionally credited with founding the Vijayanagara Empire. Who were they?
questions.explanation: Harihara I and Bukka Raya I, of the Sangama dynasty, founded the Vijayanagara Empire in 1336.

questions.q: Under which celebrated ruler did the Vijayanagara Empire reach its peak of power and prosperity?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya (reigned 1509-1529) presided over Vijayanagara's golden age of military and cultural greatness.

questions.q: The Vittala Temple at Hampi is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The Vittala Temple honours Vitthala, a form of Vishnu (Krishna), widely worshipped in the Deccan.

questions.q: The Vittala Temple is famous for pillars that produce what when struck?
questions.explanation: Hampi's Vittala Temple is renowned for its 'musical pillars' that resonate with musical tones when tapped.

questions.q: What is the Lotus Mahal at Hampi believed to have been?
options: A pavilion in the royal women's quarters
questions.explanation: The Lotus Mahal was an elegant pavilion within the zenana, the secluded enclosure for the royal women.

questions.q: The large arched stone stables near the zenana at Hampi were built to house which animals?
questions.explanation: The Elephant Stables, a row of domed chambers, sheltered the royal ceremonial elephants of Vijayanagara.

questions.q: The towering entrance structures of Hampi's great temples are known by what South Indian term?
questions.explanation: The tall, tiered temple gateway-towers in Dravidian architecture are called gopurams.

questions.q: Hampi's Krishna Temple was built by Krishnadevaraya to commemorate his victory over which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya built the Krishna Temple around 1513 to mark his conquest of Udayagiri in the Kalinga campaign.

questions.q: Which monolithic deity at Hampi is a giant statue carved from a single boulder, popularly linked to a man-lion form of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: The colossal Lakshmi Narasimha statue, depicting Vishnu's man-lion avatar, is carved from one massive granite block.

questions.q: The Hazara Rama Temple at Hampi is decorated with relief carvings narrating which epic?
questions.explanation: The Hazara Rama Temple's walls are covered with reliefs telling the story of the Ramayana.

questions.q: Several European travellers, such as the Portuguese, described Vijayanagara as one of the world's grandest cities. What did they especially marvel at?
questions.explanation: Visitors like Domingo Paes praised Vijayanagara's immense wealth, thriving bazaars and the scale of the city.

questions.q: The Vijayanagara Empire was decisively defeated in 1565 at which battle, leading to Hampi's sacking?
questions.explanation: At the Battle of Talikota in 1565, a coalition of Deccan sultanates crushed Vijayanagara, and Hampi was then plundered.

questions.q: Who was the powerful regent and de facto ruler of Vijayanagara killed at the Battle of Talikota in 1565?
questions.explanation: Rama Raya, the aged regent who effectively ruled Vijayanagara, was captured and killed at Talikota in 1565.

questions.q: The vast monolithic Ganesha image at Hampi carved with a thin belly girdle is popularly known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Sasivekalu Ganesha, named for resembling a mustard seed, is one of Hampi's huge monolithic Ganesha sculptures.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya, Vijayanagara's greatest king, belonged to which ruling house?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya was a ruler of the Tuluva dynasty, the third of Vijayanagara's four ruling lines.

questions.q: Which large open-water structure near the royal centre at Hampi is a stepped tank built of finely fitted black schist stone?
questions.explanation: The Pushkarani, a symmetrical stepped tank of dark schist, served the royal enclosure's water and ritual needs.

questions.q: The wheels of Hampi's celebrated Stone Chariot were originally designed to do what?
questions.explanation: The Stone Chariot's stone wheels were once able to turn, though they are now fixed to prevent damage.

questions.q: Hampi is traditionally identified in the Ramayana with which mythical kingdom of the vanaras (monkeys)?
questions.explanation: The Hampi region is associated with Kishkindha, the legendary monkey kingdom of Sugriva and Hanuman in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Approximately how long did the Vijayanagara Empire endure as a major power, from its founding to the fall of Hampi?
questions.explanation: Founded in 1336 and broken at Talikota in 1565, Vijayanagara flourished for well over two centuries.
